avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh and older wind slabs represent the main danger. In addition the danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase from the early morning. The conditions are dangerous for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities. Careful route selection is required.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the strong to storm force northerly foehn wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. In the course of the day the already large wind slabs will increase in size additionally. High-altitude starting zones will be replenished as a consequence of the wind. Here more medium-sized and large natural dry avalanches are possible.
In the typical avalanche paths the avalanches can as before reach fairly large size.
With the freezing level at 2600 metres combined with the foehn wind and solar radiation, numerous wet and moist avalanches are expected from the morning onwards, some of which may be large in isolated areas.
Single winter sport participants can release avalanches easily, including large ones. Caution is to be exercised on steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Little snow fell during the night. Over a wide area 5 to 10 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1800 m.
Over a wide area 60 to 100 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1800 m.
Until Saturday the wind will be strong over a wide area.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Some large and, in isolated cases, very large powder avalanches have been released last week. Isolated medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches have been released by people in the last two days.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.

avalancheActivity.comment:
They have received widespread snowfall of 30 to 60 cm of fresh snow, accompanied by strong winds. At higher elevations, the amounts of fresh snow are greater.
The avalanche prone locations are numerous. As a consequence of new snow and wind, deep wind slabs formed. Natural avalanches are further possible. In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation they can in isolated cases reach very large size and in some places endanger transportation routes situated at relatively high altitudes.
Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in backcountry touring.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack in all aspects.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights:
In some regions light snowfall. The wind will be moderate to strong. Significant warming to 2500 m.
The conditions remain dangerous. We recommend that you consult the most recent avalanche bulletin.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger. Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Some snow will fall on Saturday in some localities. As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,.

During the day: On wind-loaded slopes and in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place medium-sized and large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions over a wide area 40 to 80 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday.
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes.
In some places various wind slab layers are lying on old snow containing large grains.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mild. Midday and afternoon: Significant increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

bulletins.highlights: Remotely triggered and natural avalanches are possible.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Outside marked and open pistes a dangerous avalanche situation will prevail. The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Wind slabs are very prone to triggering in all aspects above the tree line, caution is to be exercised, including in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can be released in near-surface layers of the snowpack.
Additionally avalanches can also penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size on west, north and east facing slopes. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

As a consequence of the strong to storm force foehn wind from the north more natural avalanches are possible. This applies in case of releases originating from very steep leeward starting zones, in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized wet small and medium sized avalanches are possible. This applies especially on very steep slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
20 to 40 cm of snow, and up to 60 cm in some localities, has fallen since Thursday. Thus far the wind has been strong to storm force. Some snow will fall. This applies in particular in the north.
The wind will transport the new snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: On wind-loaded slopes a dangerous avalanche situation will persist. The danger of dry and moist avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind above the tree line, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant.
Along the border with France the avalanche prone locations are more widespread and the danger is greater.

On wind-loaded slopes and in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place medium-sized and, in many cases, large powder avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions 70 to 120 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday above approximately 1800 m. Here a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
The new snow of last week is lying on surface hoar in some places above the tree line. In some places various wind slab layers are lying on the soft surface of an old snowpack.
Performed ECTs and field observations have confirmed the existence of a weak snowack.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mild. Midday and afternoon: Significant increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

bulletins.highlights: Natural avalanches are to be expected.

avalancheActivity.highlights: A very dangerous avalanche situation will prevail. The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Large quantities of fresh snow and the wind-drifted snow are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. The fresh snow and the extensive wind slabs that are being formed by the strong westerly wind are very prone to triggering in all aspects above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are numerous. They are to be found also in steep terrain in areas close to the tree line.

More natural avalanches are to be expected. Avalanches can in some cases penetrate deep layers and reach large size. This applies on west, north and east facing slopes.

With the onset of the intense precipitation, the natural avalanche activity will increase. Caution is to be exercised in particular in leeward starting zones. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of natural avalanches being released will increase significantly.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow has fallen since Thursday. 20 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Saturday, this applies in particular as the day progresses. The wind will be moderate to strong. The wind will transport the new snow. Further wind slabs will form. Up to intermediate altitudes rain will fall.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

tendency.highlights:
Some snow will fall in some regions. The rain will give rise to softening of the snowpack at low and intermediate altitudes. As a consequence of a sometimes storm force wind from westerly directions, further wind slabs will form on Saturday.
The snow sport conditions outside marked and open pistes remain very dangerous. Avalanches can be released very easily. More natural avalanches are possible.

bulletins.highlights: Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for very extensive experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and great restraint.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1700 m. New snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released easily. New snow and wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. A high (level 4) danger of natural avalanches exists.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered. Large to very large natural avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

bulletins.highlights: Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for great restraint.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Outside marked and open pistes a very precarious avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow. Above the tree line avalanche prone wind slabs formed, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.
On very steep sunny slopes medium-sized natural avalanches must be expected. In some places the avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Mostly the dry avalanches are medium-sized and can be released by a single winter sport participant.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 20 to 40 cm of snow, and up to 50 cm in some localities, has fallen since Thursday. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes above the tree line. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. The meteorological conditions will foster a gradual weakening of the snowpack on sunny slopes.
The snowpack remains unstable over a wide area, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas in all altitude zones, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: The snowpack remains prone to triggering over a wide area. The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Moist and wet avalanches are possible from the morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the strong to storm force northerly foehn wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. In the course of the day the wind slabs will increase in size additionally.
Caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
With the freezing level at 2600 metres combined with the foehn wind and solar radiation, wet and moist avalanches are expected from the morning onwards, including large ones in isolated areas.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 50 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1600 m. Until Saturday the wind will be strong.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Some medium-sized to large natural dry avalanches have been released in the last few days.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs require caution. As a consequence of the solar radiation, the likelihood of dry and moist avalanches being released will increase in particular on sunny slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days.
In particular intermediate and high altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: Soft wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in areas close to the tree line. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable.
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 25 cm of snow fell on Thursday in all altitude zones. The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. Shady slopes: The snowpack is dry and has a loosely bonded surface.
Sunny slopes: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m.

tendency.highlights:
Early morning: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
On sunny slopes and in steep rocky terrain the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches will increase during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. The wind slabs can be released easily or naturally in particular on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
The fresh snow and in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.

snowpackStructure.comment: Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger. Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow significantly. As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days.
In particular intermediate and high altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: Soft wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant above the tree line. The wind slabs are clearly recognisable.
In particular steep sunny slopes and base of rock walls: Medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 25 cm of snow fell on Thursday in all altitude zones. In these regions above approximately 1200 m a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.
The strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Shady slopes: The snowpack is dry and has a loosely bonded surface.
Sunny slopes below approximately 2200 m: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mild. Midday and afternoon: Gradual increase in danger of dry and moist avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs are to be avoided. Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind will be strong over a wide area. Over a wide area large surface-area wind slabs will form. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. They can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign. The avalanche prone locations are quite prevalent and are easy to recognise. Experience and restraint are required. As the day progresses as a consequence of solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of moist avalanches.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow of Thursday is lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. In particular above approximately 1900 m sometimes large wind slabs formed. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

In addition the wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are prone to triggering in some cases still. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found on north, east and southeast facing slopes above the tree line.

As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of natural wet avalanches being released will increase a little.

Restraint is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Thursday. 10 to 20 cm of snow will fall on Saturday. The wind will be strong to storm force in some localities.
The sometimes strong wind will transport the new snow. Sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be in some cases prone to triggering. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Gullies and bowls where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Avalanches can as before be released, in particular by large loads and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment: In the last few days sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. In some places wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and on wind-loaded slopes small and medium-sized slab avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and wind. Single backcountry tourers can release avalanches in some places, including medium-sized ones.
avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack will consolidate.
